title: How Winners of the 'Green Nobel' Are Protecting the Environment | Scientific American
impact_evidence: Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "Mumba organized a lawsuit against a U.K.-based mining company for river pollution and other environmental damage in Zambia's Copperbelt Province, where one of the company's major copper-mining subsidiaries operated." || Environmental | Biodiversity & Ecosystems | Impacts on aquatic ecosystems and life below water -> "I went to a place I used to go when I was young to fish, and then I found there were simply no fish," || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Accountability for accidents and environmental damage -> "It sets an example for companies to be held liable when they pollute the environment, that's No. 1." | "The company, Vedanta Resources Ltd., agreed to settle the claims without admission of liability in 2021." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "In 2019, the United Kingdom's Supreme Court ruled that the case could be heard in British courts, setting a legal precedent that has since been used against Shell PLC's operations in Nigeria." | "I think [the law] can have significant influence on that company's operations to make sure they maintain duty of care in the places they operate." || Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> "I think it has made those communities more aware of their rights. I can say that those villages thought it would be a sheer waste of time to take this on. I think everybody now knows that there's recourse when such things occur."
